name: Mercury soil/water sensor (MerR + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: Detects ionic mercury Hg(II) in soil and water. The MerR/PmerT sensing module gates a CRISPRi circuit driving a visible chromoprotein, in the rugged environmental chassis P. putida KT2440.

input.operatingRange: MerR-based sensors respond from sub-µM to µM Hg(II)

sensing.sensorMechanism: MerR binds Hg(II) and switches the PmerT promoter from repressed to activated (a distorted-operator activation mechanism).
sensing.signalFlow: Hg(II) -> MerR activates PmerT -> PmerT transcribes an sgRNA -> sgRNA + dCas9-activator recruit RNA polymerase to a weak reporter promoter (CRISPRa) -> amilCP chromoprotein is produced -> visible blue-purple colour (BUFFER/amplifier).

parts.name: Reporter-targeting sgRNA
parts.source: designed to target the weak reporter promoter for CRISPRa

parts.name: dCas9-ω activator
parts.source: Bikard et al. 2013; Dong et al. 2018 (CRISPRa)
parts.notes: dCas9 fused to RNAP omega subunit for transcriptional activation.

parts.name: amilCP chromoprotein
parts.notes: Blue-purple pigment, visible to the naked eye (no instrument).

output.readout: Visible blue-purple pigment (naked eye / smartphone camera)
output.result: Cells/colonies turn blue-purple when mercury is present.

performance.dynamicRange: ~0.1-10 µM Hg(II) for native MerR modules
performance.notes: MerR mercury sensors and CRISPRa are validated separately in the literature; the integrated CRISPRa device is a design template.

safety.biocontainment: P. putida KT2440 is a certified HV1 safety host; pair with a containment circuit for field use.
safety.notes: Pigment output needs no instrument, suited to low-resource field testing.

buildSteps.title: Design the CRISPRa sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target a window upstream of a weak reporter promoter for activation; verify host off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: MerR + PmerT -> sgRNA. TU2: dCas9-omega. TU3: weak promoter -> amilCP. Clone into SEVA vectors for P. putida.

buildSteps.title: Transform P. putida KT2440
buildSteps.description: Electroporate and select; confirm low background pigment without mercury.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Incubate with the soil eluate / water sample plus a Hg(II) standard curve.

buildSteps.title: Score colour
buildSteps.description: Compare pigment to the standard curve by eye or camera.

provenance.source: Design template combining a validated MerR/PmerT mercury-sensing module with a dCas9 CRISPRa reporter circuit
partsSources: mer operon / MerR mercury biosensors (e.g. Selifonova et al. 1993)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
